question: Design LFU Cache with Counter-Based Replacement
description:
Implement a Least Frequently Used (LFU) cache with per-line frequency counters.

' +
        'Increment the counter on each hit. On miss, evict the line with lowest frequency. Handle counter saturation and define a tie-breaking strategy.

' +
        '**Example:**
' +
        '```
Fill lines A (freq=3) and B (freq=1)
Miss on C → Evicts B (lower frequency)
```

' +
        '**Constraints:**
' +
        '- Configurable counter width (e.g., 4 bits)
' +
        '- Counters saturate at max, do not wrap
' +
        '- Deterministic tie-breaking required
requirements: COUNTER MANAGEMENT: Maintain one frequency counter per cache line. Define counter width (e.g., 4 bits) and saturation behavior at maximum value (hold at max, do not wrap).
INCREMENT POLICY: On cache hit, increment the frequency counter for the accessed line. On cache miss with refill, initialize new line counter to 1 (or 0, document your choice).
EVICTION SELECTION: On cache miss requiring eviction, find the line with minimum frequency count. If multiple lines have the same minimum frequency, apply tie-breaking rule.
TIE-BREAKING: Define deterministic tie-breaker when multiple lines have minimum frequency. Options: (1) use LRU timestamp among tied lines, (2) select lowest way index, (3) other documented policy.
COUNTER RESET: On line invalidation or replacement, reset counter to initial value. On line fill, set counter appropriately.
EDGE CASES: Handle all-invalid cache (cold start), counter saturation, all counters at same value.
TEST CASE 1 - Cold All-Invalid: All ways invalid in a set. Expected: victim_way selects lowest index (way 0) for allocation.
TEST CASE 2 - Counter Saturation: With 2-bit counters (max=3), after refill then repeated hits on one way. Expected: that way's counter saturates at 3 without wrapping.
TEST CASE 3 - LFU Victim: All ways valid; one way has strictly lower frequency than others. Expected: victim_way is the minimum-frequency way (lowest index on ties).
TEST CASE 4 - Invalid Way Priority: At least one invalid way exists. Expected: victim_way is the lowest-index invalid way, ignoring LFU among valid lines.
TEST CASE 5 - Tie All Minimum: All valid ways share the same minimum frequency. Expected: deterministic tie-break selects lowest way index.
TEST CASE 6 - Multi-Set Independence: Repeat cold victim check on a different req_set. Expected: victim_way is still lowest invalid for that set; set-0 state does not corrupt set-1 choice.
hints: Saturation: use conditional increment (if counter < MAX).
Consider periodic global decay (right-shift all counters) to prevent saturation.
